result:
Research complete. I read four authoritative primary sources (three Adobe Experience League AEMaaCS docs + Adobe's official headless client repo) and captured the structured findings.

Key takeaways for your stack (AEMaaCS, pure headless, standalone React, dev token):

- **Delivery path**: persist queries on author, execute via cacheable `GET /graphql/execute.json/<config>/<label>` with semicolon-separated, URL-encoded variables — not ad-hoc POST (blocked on publish by default).
- **React client**: `@adobe/aem-headless-client-js` → `runPersistedQuery('<config>/<label>', variables)`, with the dev token passed as the `auth` Bearer string from env.
- **Filtering**: scope with `_path` first; only top-level scalars + `_path` filter efficiently at the JCR level — nested/multiline/virtual-field filters and sorts run in-memory over the whole result set.
- **Biggest silent trap**: `execute.json` returns HTTP 200 even on errors unless you flip the OSGi `responseContentTypeGraphQLResponseJson` flag.

All details are in the structured fields above.

structured_output.domain: AEM GraphQL API (persisted queries, endpoints, filtering) — AEM as a Cloud Service, pure-headless Content Fragments delivered via GraphQL to a standalone external React SPA authenticated with a dev/service token.
key_apis: Auto-generated query types per Content Fragment Model: `<model>ByPath(_path, variation)` returns a single `item`; `<model>List(filter, sort, offset, limit, _assetTransform)` returns `items`; `<model>Paginated(filter, sort, first, after)` returns `edges { cursor node }` + `pageInfo { endCursor hasNextPage }` (forward-only).

Persisted query EXECUTE (production, GET, cacheable): `GET /graphql/execute.json/<config>/<persisted-label>` — e.g. `/graphql/execute.json/wknd/plain-article-query`. Variables are appended after the path, separated by semicolons: `...;var=value;var2=value2`.

Persisted query AUTHORING (author tier): create with `PUT /graphql/persist.json/<config>/<label>`; update with `POST /graphql/persist.json/<config>/<label>`. Stored under `/conf/<config>/settings/graphql/persistentQueries/<label>` (global stored under `/conf/global/...`).
Ad-hoc POST endpoint (dev/test only): `POST /content/cq:graphql/<config>/endpoint.json`; schema introspection via the same endpoint with `.GQLschema`. POST is blocked on publish by default.

Filter argument shape: `filter: { <field>: { _expressions: [{ value, _operator }], _logOp: AND|OR } }`; operators include EQUALS/EQUALS_NOT, CONTAINS/CONTAINS_NOT, STARTS_WITH, GREATER(_EQUAL)/LOWER(_EQUAL), AT/NOT_AT/BEFORE/AFTER; modifiers `_ignoreCase`, `_sensitiveness`, `_apply` (e.g. ALL_OR_EMPTY).

System/virtual fields: `_path`, `_variation` (returns variation name or `master`, with master fallback), `_metadata`, `_model`, `_tags`, `_references`; on image refs `_dynamicUrl` / `_authorUrl` and the `_assetTransform` arg (format GIF/PNG/JPG/WEBP, size, crop, rotation, quality, seoName) for web-optimized renditions.

@adobe/aem-headless-client-js: `new AEMHeadless({ serviceURL, endpoint, auth })`; `runPersistedQuery(path, [variables], [options], [retryOptions]) => Promise`; also `persistQuery`, `listPersistedQueries`, `runQuery`. `auth` is a Bearer-token string (dev token) or a `[user, pass]` pair.
idioms: Production rule of thumb: author/test queries in GraphiQL on author, persist them, and let the React app call the GET `execute.json` endpoint — persisted GETs are cacheable at Dispatcher + CDN; ad-hoc POST is not and is disabled on publish.

In the React SPA, instantiate one AEMHeadless client and wrap each query in a `fetchPersistedQuery` helper that calls `runPersistedQuery('<config>/<label>', variables)`; pass variables as a plain object — the SDK handles the `;name=value` URL encoding for you.

Parameterize persisted queries with GraphQL variables (e.g. `query($path: String!) { adventureByPath(_path: $path) { item {...} } }`) so the same persisted query is reused with different inputs instead of building strings client-side.

Configure dev auth via env (e.g. `REACT_APP_DEV_TOKEN` / `.env.development`) and pass it as the `auth` Bearer string; the Cloud Console-issued local development token is the standard way to reach a remote AEMaaCS instance from a standalone app.
Scope every list/filter query with `_path` first so the JCR pre-filter shrinks the result set before in-memory filtering runs.

Use a global endpoint/config for models shared across sites; use a site-specific config (e.g. `wknd`) when models and queries belong to one Sites configuration — the persisted query's config must match the model's config.
Request only the rich-text formats you need (plaintext vs html vs markdown vs json) to keep payloads small; prefer cursor-based `...Paginated` for deep paging.
gotchas: Filtering/sorting on NESTED fragment fields or on multiline (HTML/Markdown/JSON) fields, virtual `_`-fields (except `_path`), CONTAINS_NOT, `_ignoreCase`, null checks, and non-zero `_sensitiveness` all happen IN MEMORY after loading the entire model's result set — slow on large datasets. Only top-level scalar fields and `_path` are filtered efficiently at the JCR level.

POST GraphQL is blocked by default on AEMaaCS publish; you must set the Cloud Manager env var `ENABLE_GRAPHQL_ENDPOINT=true` to allow it — but the recommended path is persisted GET queries, not enabling POST.

`execute.json` returns HTTP 200 even when the query errors, by default. Set the OSGi 'Persisted Query Service Configuration' flag `responseContentTypeGraphQLResponseJson=true` to get real 400/404/500 status codes — otherwise client error handling silently misses failures.

Variables in the GET URL are semicolon-separated and must be UTF-8 encoded when sent raw: `;`=`%3B`, `=`=`%3D`, `/`=`%2F` (paths in variable values are full of slashes). Forgetting to encode breaks the request.

A persisted query is bound to its config: a query saved under `/conf/wknd/...` is distinct from one under `/conf/global/...` even with identical text, and must be executed against the matching `<config>` segment.
`_variation` field cannot be combined with the `includeVariations` filter in the same query; a missing variation silently falls back to `master`.

Pagination is forward-only (`first`/`after`); no backward cursor. `offset`/`limit` on `...List` degrades on high offsets because it still loads the full result set into memory — switch to `...Paginated` for later pages.
Keep Content Fragment nesting under ~5–6 levels; deep nesting forces full-hierarchy traversal and large memory loads.

Browser CORS: a standalone external React app hitting AEMaaCS needs CORS configured server-side (CORS OSGi policy / Dispatcher) and a token with appropriate scope; the dev token is for development/non-production, not a long-lived production secret.
structured_output.version_notes: Facts are current for AEM as a Cloud Service (continuously released; docs verified June 2026). Cursor pagination (`...Paginated`, `first`/`after`, `edges`/`pageInfo`) and the hybrid JCR-then-in-memory filtering model are AEMaaCS/6.5.x-era features. Default persisted-query cache headers on publish: max-age 60s, CDN s-maxage 7200s, stale-while-revalidate 86400s, stale-if-error 86400s — overridable at persist time via cache-control/surrogate-control. Paginated default `first` is 50 (max 100). The same GraphQL API exists in AEM 6.5 (on-prem) but endpoint enablement, dev tokens, and CDN behavior differ from Cloud Service; this research targets AEMaaCS. @adobe/aem-headless-client-js referenced around v2.1.x.

sources.title: AEM GraphQL API for use with Content Fragments — AEM as a Cloud Service
Persisted GraphQL queries — AEM as a Cloud Service
Optimizing GraphQL queries — AEM as a Cloud Service
